macro_segments.title: Introduction to Shvuos Ha'eidus: Basic Scenarios and Obligation for Korban

micro_segments.title: Opening and transition from Shvuos Ha'eidus parameters to practical application
Mishnah: The basic scenario of Shvuos Ha'eidus - requesting testimony and false oath
Two ways to trigger Shvuos Ha'eidus: direct oath or implied through amen response
Distinction between Mushpa mipi atzmo and Mushpa mipi acherim based on amen response

macro_segments.title: The Requirement for Denial in Beis Din and Location-Based Chiyuv

micro_segments.title: Case where oath taken outside Beis Din five times, admission in Beis Din results in exemption
Principle that denial (kfira) in Beis Din is necessary to trigger chiyuv from prior oaths
Retraction principle (kivan she'higid shuv eino chozer): inability to recant after denial in Beis Din
Case of multiple oaths in Beis Din: only one korban despite five oaths due to kfira
Rabbi Shimon's reason: after first denial in Beis Din, witnesses become posul eidus

macro_segments.title: Two Witnesses: Simultaneous vs. Sequential Denials and the Chiyuv Structure

micro_segments.title: Both witnesses deny simultaneously: both are chayav
Sequential denial: first witness chayav, second witness patur due to being ed echad
First denies, second admits: first is chayav for withholding testimony
Two groups of witnesses: both chayav even if sequential because each group can independently testify

macro_segments.title: The Requirement to Hear Demand Directly from the Litigant

micro_segments.title: Shmuel's teaching: witnesses must hear verbalized demand from the litigant himself
Running after witnesses does not constitute sufficient demand; verbal request required
Gemara questions whether Shmuel's teaching is necessary given the mishnah's wording
Comparison to Shvuos Ha'pikadon to show that amar in mishnah may not be davka requirement
Resolution: amar is just describing typical scenario; Shmuel clarifies verbal demand is essential
Braisa corroborates: witnesses see litigant pursuing, take oath preemptively without hearing demand

macro_segments.title: Scriptural Basis and Logical Derivation of Requirements

micro_segments.title: Gemara derives location requirement in Beis Din from im lo yagid (if does not testify) pasuk
Abaye's interpretation: denial must occur where testimony could actually be given (Beis Din)
Rav Papa's objection: if pasuk requires Beis Din context, does it apply to oath itself too?
Derivation of multiple chiyuvim from ve'haya ki ye'ashem le'achas me'eila teaching
Logical reconciliation: multiple oaths outside Beis Din, but denial that triggers must be inside

macro_segments.title: Two Witnesses: Simultaneous vs. Sequential Denials and the Chiyuv Structure (continued)

micro_segments.title: Gemara explores whether simultaneous is literal or within k'dei dibur (speech time)

macro_segments.title: The Debate of Rabbi Eliezer ben Rabbi Shimon vs. Chachamim on Ed Echad

micro_segments.title: Gemara identifies the conceptual debate: does ed echad obligate shevua or mamon?
Rabbi Eliezer's position: davar ha'gorem l'mamon k'mamon dami - indirect path to mamon counts
Chachamim position: only direct mamon obligation triggers Shvuos Ha'eidus chiyuv
Abaye's cryptic statement: cases where both opinions agree on chayav and patur
Sotah witnesses: ed tumah (actual sex witness) everyone agrees chayav; kinuy witnesses all agree patur
Sotah stirah witnesses (went into seclusion): debates Rabbi Eliezer vs. Chachamim on indirect mamon
Cases where shvua cannot be taken: both agree ed echad chayav because chiyuv becomes chiyuv mamon
Rabbi Abba's case: grabber admits taking but claims it was his; can't swear falsely, must pay

macro_segments.title: Application to Death Testimony and Final Clarifications

micro_segments.title: Rav Papa's parallel framework using death of husband testimony with similar case divisions
Case where witness tells woman privately but refuses in Beis Din: patur because testimony already gave effect
Case where witness refuses both privately and in Beis Din: chayav because immediate monetary consequence
Conclusion: Shvuos Ha'eidus chiyuv requires ability to have real monetary effect

topical_tags.term: Kivan she'higid shuv eino chozer u'magid
Mushpa mipi atzmo vs. mipi acherim
Im lo yagid (if does not testify)
Rabbi Eliezer ben Rabbi Shimon vs. Chachamim
